TypeError: _BatchNormBase.__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'eps'

llm_operation.reason: PyTorch error 'running_mean should contain 3 elements not 256' indicates num_features (256) mismatches input channel dimension (3) — torch.nn.BatchNorm2d expects num_features == input.shape[1]. PaddlePaddle error '_BatchNormBase.__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'eps'' shows parameter name mismatch: Paddle uses 'epsilon', not 'eps'. Also, Paddle's 'affine' equivalent is controlled via 'weight_attr' and 'bias_attr', not a boolean 'affine' flag. Per Paddle docs, 'affine=False' maps to weight_attr=False and bias_attr=False. Additionally, Paddle's default momentum is 0.9 (not 0.1), but it accepts 0.1; however, PyTorch momentum=0.1 is valid. So repair: (1) fix num_features=3 to match input shape[1]=3; (2) rename 'eps' → 'epsilon' in Paddle; (3) replace 'affine': false with 'weight_attr': false, 'bias_attr': false in Paddle; (4) keep other params consistent and valid.

执行状态为compared，PyTorch和PaddlePaddle均执行成功且结果一致，符合变异（fuzzing）条件。为提升测试鲁棒性与边界覆盖，需对原始用例进行变异：将输入shape从[2,3,4,4]改为含边界值的[1,1,1,1]（最小合法4D batch），同时将eps设为文档允许的极小值1e-8（PyTorch默认1e-5，Paddle默认1e-5，但均支持更小值），momentum设为0.0（合法边界，对应无动量更新），并显式设置track_running_stats=False（PyTorch）与use_global_stats=False（Paddle）以验证统计模式一致性；此外，affine=False在PyTorch中对应weight_attr=False & bias_attr=False在Paddle中已存在，但需补充track_running_stats/use_global_stats控制项以覆盖更多行为分支。

ValueError: Expected more than 1 value per channel when training, got input size torch.Size([1, 1, 1, 1])

llm_operation.reason: PyTorch's BatchNorm2d raises 'Expected more than 1 value per channel when training' for input size [1, 1, 1, 1] because batch norm requires at least 2 samples per channel to compute meaningful batch statistics (mean/variance) during training; this is a documented constraint in PyTorch. PaddlePaddle allows it (with use_global_stats=False), but PyTorch enforces minimum batch size > 1 per channel in training mode. To make the test valid and pass in both frameworks, we must ensure the batch dimension (N) is ≥2, or switch to eval mode. Since track_running_stats=False and no running stats are tracked, the only reliable fix is to increase batch size to 2 — this satisfies PyTorch's requirement while preserving identical semantics (no affine, no tracking, same epsilon/momentum). The minimal repair is changing input shape from [1,1,1,1] to [2,1,1,1].
